steps.title: Expand each part
steps.content: First expand the squared binomial and the linear terms: $$(2x+4)^2=4x^2+16x+16,$$ $$-(x-3)=-x+3,$$ and $$2(x+5)=2x+10.$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Add the parts together: $$4x^2+16x+16-x+3+2x+10.$$ The $x$ terms give $16x-x+2x=17x$, and the constants give $16+3+10=29$. The expression is $$4x^2+17x+29,$$ so A is correct.
